content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 8, 1992

Honorable Edward E. Munger
Planning Commissioner
Fresno County Planning Commission
5588 North Palm Avenue
Fresno, CA 93704-1913

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-92-365

Dear Commissioner Munger:

This is in response to your letter requesting advice regard-
ing your responsibilities as a member of the Fresno County
Planning Commission under the conflict-of-interest provisions of
the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

Please note that nothing in this letter should be construed
to evaluate any conduct which may have already taken place. In
addition, this letter is based on the facts presented to us. The
Commission does not act as the finder of fact in providing advice.
(In re Ogelsby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

May you participate in the Fresno County Planning
Commission's consideration of the development of property known as
Ball Ranch?

CONCLUSION

Absent facts to indicate that the Ball Ranch project will
have a material financial effect on PALMIA or some other economic
interest, you may participate in the Ball Ranch decisions.

FACTS

You are a member of the Fresno County Planning Commission.
On July 9, 1992, the planning commission will be considering a
general plan amendment and specific plan proposal for an area of

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Our File No. A-92-365
Page 2


the county known as Ball Ranch.  Ball Ranch is approximately 400
feet from 2,800 acres of land owned by Knox and Carol Blassingame.

     In November 1990, you invested $10,000 in PALMIA, a
California limited partnership, constituting 1.923 percent of the
partnership.  PALMIA was formed for the purpose of the $500,000 to
be used to secure an option to purchase the Blassingame property.
You stated that you have no involvement in the daily operation of
the partnership.  You stated that if the partnership is unable to
raise funds needed to purchase the property, the partnership will
be dissolved and you will lose your interest.

     On July 2, 1992, you received written notice from the General
Partner of PALMIA that the option had expired and at the current
time, PALMIA had no legal interest in the Blassingame property.

                              ANALYSIS

Economic Interests

     Section 87100 prohibits any public official from making,
participating in making, or otherwise using his official position
to influence a governmental decision in which the official has a
financial interest.  As a Fresno County Planning Commissioner, you
are a public official under the Act.  (Section 8204B.)

     Section 87103 provides in pertinent part:

          An official has a financial interest in a
     decision within the meaning of Section 87100 if it
     is reasonably foreseeable that the decision will
     have a material financial effect, distinguishable
     from its effect on the public generally, on the
     official or a member of his or her immediate family
     or on:

               (a)  Any business entity in which the
          public official has a direct or indirect
          investment worth one thousand dollars ($1,000)
          or more.

                                * * *

               (d)  Any business entity in which the
          public official is a director, officer,
          partner, trustee, employee, or holds any
          position of management.

     Section 87103(a) and (d).

Our File No. A-92-365
Page 3


     You are a limited partner in PALMIA, a partnership created to
purchase property in Fresno County.2  Consequently, PALMIA is an
economic interest and you are required to disqualify yourself from
any decision if it is reasonably foreseeable3 that the decision
will have a material financial effect on PALMIA, distinguishable
from the effect on the public generally.

     The Commission has adopted regulations which provide
guidelines to determine whether the effect of a governmental
decision is material.  Where a business entity in which the
official has a financial interest is directly involved in a
decision before the official's agency, the official may not
participate.  (Regulation 18702.1(a)).4  PALMIA is not directly
involved in the Ball Ranch decisions.

     However, even if PALMIA is not directly involved in the Ball
Ranch decisions, you are still required to disqualify yourself
from participating in the decisions if they will indirectly have a
material financial effect on the partnership.  Regulation 18702.2
provides different thresholds of materiality based on the
financial size of the business entity.

     For a relatively small business entity, Regulation 18702.2(g)
provides that the effect of a decision is material where:

          (1)  The decision will result in an increase
     or decrease in the gross revenues for a fiscal year
     of $10,000 or more; or


_________________
2  If your interest in PALMIA was 10 percent or greater, you would
also have a pro-rata interest in any investments or real property
held by PALMIA, as well as income to PALMIA.  You stated your
interest in the partnership is less that 2 percent.

3  Whether the financial consequences of a decision are reasonably
foreseeable to the time a governmental decision is made depends on
the facts of each particular case.  An effect is considered
reasonably foreseeable if there is a substantial likelihood that
it will occur.  Certainty is not required.  However, if an effect
is only a mere possibility, it is not reasonably foreseeable.  (In
re Thorner (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 198.)

4  A business entity is directly involved in a decision before the
planning commission when the business either personally or by an
agent initiates the proceeding in which the decision will be made
by filing an application, claim, appeal, or similar request, or is
a named party in, or is the subject of, the proceeding concerning
the decision before the official or the official's agency.
(Regulation 18702.1(b).)  For example, if the decision involved a
development project owned by PALMIA, PALMIA would be directly
before the planning commission and you could not participate.

Our File No. A-92-365
Page 4


          (2)  The decision will result in the business
     entity incurring or avoiding additional expenses or
     reducing or eliminating existing expenses for a
     fiscal year in the amount of $2,500 or more; or

          (3)  The decision will result in the increase
     or decrease in the value of assets or liabilities
     of $10,000 or more.

     Thus, if subdivision (g) is the appropriate standard, you
will be required to disqualify yourself if the decisions could
foreseeably increase or decrease the gross revenues, assets or
liabilities of the partnership by $10,000 or more, or increase or
decrease expenses by $2,500.

     At the time that you first requested advice, PALMIA had an
option to purchase the Blassingame property.  Thus, it was
foreseeable that the Ball Ranch decisions would affect property
near the property interest of PALMIA and therefore would have
some financial effect on PALMIA--possibly a material financial
effect.

     Since that time, however, PALMIA has forfeited all interest
in the Blassingame property.  Thus, there are no facts to indicate
that the decision on the Ball Ranch applications will have any
effect on PALMIA.  Consequently, absent some financial effect on
PALMIA caused by the Ball Ranch project, you will not be
prohibited from participating in the decisions.5

     If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please feel free to contact me at (916) 322-5901.6

                                   Sincerely,

                                   Scott Hallabrin
                                   Acting General Counsel

                                   By:  John W. Wallace
                                        Counsel, Legal Division

SH:JWW:aa

_______________________
     5  You also noted in your letter that if PALMIA was unable to
raise sufficient funds to purchase the Blassingame property, the
partnership would be dissolved and you would lose your interest.
If this is the case, you would no longer have an investment
interest in PALMIA to create a conflict of interest.

     6  Copies of Commission regulations and Opinions are available in
many law libraries.  Alternatively, copies of these materials and
Commission advice letters may be obtained from the commission at a
cost of 10¢ per page.

have
a material financial effect on
PALMIA or some other economic
interest,
you may participate in the Ball Ranch decisions.
